extraction.one_line_summary: IMPEY Daryl won after holding a late two-up breakaway with TEUNS Dylan, refusing to pull through the final kilometer then outssprinting the Belgian.
tactical_patterns: PÖSTLBERGER Lukas attacked solo from 14-rider break and held 30s gap alone, nearly succeeding against cooperative chasers.
IMPEY sat on TEUNS final kilometer forcing Belgian to pace, then won sprint—experience trumped youthful strength in two-up scenario.
ROCHE Nicolas sitting on in chase group demoralized chasers, making continued commitment psychologically impossible despite small gap.
Early break succeeded on Bastille Day as predicted—peloton motivation drops day-after when previous break wins, creating tactical opportunity window.

rider_signals.IMPEY Daryl: positive: tactical maturity elite—refused pulls final km forcing younger TEUNS to work, conserved energy for winning sprint
rider_signals.PÖSTLBERGER Lukas: positive: nearly soloed to victory from 14-rider break, holding 30s alone against organized chase revealing exceptional diesel engine
rider_signals.TEUNS Dylan: negative: inexperience showed—allowed IMPEY free ride final kilometer then lost sprint, lacked tactical ruthlessness to force cooperation
rider_signals.ROCHE Nicolas: neutral: sitting on in chase discouraged group cooperation but ultimately prevented podium finish
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: neutral: chased for third after front duo escaped, suggesting decent form but lacked acceleration to bridge critical gap
rider_signals.NAESEN Oliver: neutral: bridged to break after initial split, showing aggressive intent but faded in finale
rider_signals.HERRADA Jesús: neutral: present in break and mentioned in finale chase but no decisive action
rider_signals.BOASSON HAGEN Edvald: neutral: made breakaway but mentioned only in passing, no impact on outcome
